rulings.Case Title: LEANDRO ROSALES et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 23, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant RPI Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD ROUTT VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 23, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 4, Defendant Certainteed Corp.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BILLY COLTON VS. CERTAINEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Specially Appearing Defendant The Howard P. Foley Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 23, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Specially appearing defendant The Howard P. Foley Company's Motion to Quash Service of Summons is denied. Plaintiff satisfied his burden in demonstrating that service of summons was proper pursuant to C.C.P. § 416.20 and Corp. Code §§ 2114(c), 2110(a). Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A, B, C, and D. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY HALEY et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Teresa Haley As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complain

No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
